title: A heliostat
abstract: A heliostat (10) including a flexible member defining a plurality of nodes (14) between a plurality of reflective elements (16) mounted on the tarp (12), a lifting means (20) connected to the tarp (12) for lifting a portion of the tarp (12) to a height above a ground surface (22), tensioning members (24) connected to the plurality of nodes (14) at one end of the tarp (12) for allowing the tarp (12) to be tensioned when opposing ends of the cables (24) are connected to an anchoring surface (26), and an adjustment means (28) for adjusting the tension of the tensioning members (24) causing the reflective surface of the tarp (12) to change shape to allow sunlight (30) hitting the reflective surfaces (18) of the reflective elements (16) to be reflected towards a desired target surface (32).
claims:
Claims 1. A heliostat including: - a flexible member defining a plurality of nodes wherein at least a portion of a surface of the flexible member is reflective; a lifting means connected to the flexible member for lifting a portion of the flexible member to a height above a ground surface; tensioning members connected to the plurality of nodes defined by the flexible member at one end for allowing the flexible member to be tensioned when opposing ends of the tensioning members are connected to an anchoring surface; and an adjustment means for adjusting the tension of the tensioning members causing the reflective surface of the flexible member to change shape to allow sunlight hitting the reflective surface to be reflected towards a desired target surface. 2. A heliostat as claimed in claim 1 wherein the flexible member includes a plurality of reflective elements mounted thereon so as to define the reflective surface. 3. A heliostat as claimed in claim 1 or 2 wherein the flexible member is in the form of a tarp. 4. A heliostat as claimed in claim 3 wherein the tarp is manufactured from any suitable material which is selected from the group including basalt fibre, basalt fibre mesh, nylon, PVC, and air-tight material. 5. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the flexible member has a generally parabolic shape for allowing sunlight to be reflected by the reflective elements towards and onto the target surface. 6. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the flexible member includes a permeable portion for allowing wind to pass therethrough to reduce wind forces exerted on the flexible member. 7. A heliostat as claimed in claim 6 wherein the permeable portion of the flexible member is reflective. 8. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 2 to 7 wherein the reflective element is manufactured from a lightweight flexible material. 9. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 2 to 8 wherein the reflective element is in the form of tensioned film. 10. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 2 to 9 wherein the reflective element is preformed into a shape which serves to compensate for the change in shape thereof as a result of being lifted by the lifting means in use. 11. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 2 to 10 wherein holes are defined in the reflective elements for reducing forces exerted thereon by wind. 12. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 2 to 11 wherein each reflective element has a surface area in the range of 15m 2 to 40m 2 . 13. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein a supporting structure is provided for supporting the reflective elements on the flexible member. 14. A heliostat as claimed in claim 13 wherein the supporting structure includes a supporting member on which the reflective element is mounted, and a supporting 

framework for supporting the supporting member, and with it, the reflective element, on the flexible member. 15. A heliostat as claimed in claim 14 wherein the supporting member has a generally concave shape. 16. A heliostat as claimed in claim 14 wherein the supporting member has a double parabolic shape in order to maximize the amount of sunlight to be received and reflected by the reflective elements. 17. A heliostat as claimed in claim 14 wherein the supporting framework includes a plurality of supports which are arranged in a grid-like manner. 18. A heliostat as claimed in claim 17 wherein the supports are manufactured from layers of various suitable composite materials wherein the composite materials having a higher strength and density are utilised for supports located towards a periphery of the reflective element and wherein layers of lower strength and density are utilised for supports located generally centrally relative the reflective element for reducing the overall weight of the supporting structure. 19. A heliostat as claimed in claim 17 or 18 wherein the supports are manufactured from any suitable lightweight synthetics, plastics or metallic material. 20. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 17 to 19 wherein the supports are manufactured from any one of the group including polystyrene, expanded polystyrene, Geofoamâ¢, and titanium. 21. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 13 to 20 wherein the supporting structure includes foot members which are shaped and configured to allow the supporting framework to rest on the flexible member. 22. A heliostat as claimed in claim 21 wherein the foot members are located at substantially equal distances from a centre point of the reflective element for allowing the parabolic shape of the flexible member to be maintained in use. 23. A heliostat as claimed in claim 21 or 22 wherein the foot members have a generally dome shape wherein a flat portion of the dome is attached to the supporting framework so as to ensure that contact points between foot members of a single reflective element and the flexible member are always the same distance to the centre point of the reflective element. 24. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 21 to 23 wherein the foot members are connected to the supporting framework via spring members for allowing a constant force to be applied on the flexible member. 25. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 21 to 24 wherein the foot members are extendable, thereby capable of applying variable force as required to ensure that the reflective element is orientated as desired. 26. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the nodes are in the form of layers of material stacked on top of each other for strengthening thereof. 27. A heliostat as claimed in claim 26 wherein the node tapers in thickness away from a point at which the tensioning member is connected to the node for accommodating stress concentrations at the node as a result of being connected to the tensioning members. 28. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein wind- tight fabric overlays the flexible member on an operatively rearward side thereof. 29. A heliostat as claimed in claim 28 wherein the wind tight fabric includes holes defined therein for permitting passage of wind at predetermined locations, thereby directing the wind away from the supporting structure. 30. A heliostat as claimed in claim 29 wherein the holes lead to generally cylindrical sleeves manufactured from wind tight fabric for allowing the wind to pass through the flexible member while minimising the wind forces exerted on the supporting structure. 31 . A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the lifting means is in the form of a balloon which is filled with a gas, which gas is lighter than air so as to provide a degree of lift. 32. A heliostat as claimed in claim 31 wherein a plurality of balloons is connected to the flexible member along a width thereof. 33. A heliostat as claimed in claim 31 or 32 wherein balloons located towards peripheral regions of the flexible member are smaller than the balloons located towards a generally central region of the flexible member so as to increase lifting forces of the balloons over the generally central region of the flexible member. 34. A heliostat as claimed in claim 31 wherein the gas is in the form of any suitable gas with a density that is less than that of air or any suitable heated gas. 35. A heliostat as claimed in claim 34 wherein the gas is in the form of hydrogen. 36. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 31 to 35 wherein the balloons have a generally elongate cylindrical shape. 37. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 31 to 36 wherein an operatively lower end region of the balloon is of a generally cone shape. 38. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the claims 31 to 37 wherein the balloons have a diameter in the range of 3m to 7m. 39. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the lifting means includes buoyant aerofoils which are fastened to the flexible member or the balloons for providing additional lift when wind speeds increase. 40. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the tensioning members are connected to the plurality of nodes on either side of the flexible member, the tensioning members being connected to and extend from the nodes on both sides of the flexible member. 41. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the tensioning members are in the form of any one of the group including a rope, cable, tether, basalt fibre and wire. 42. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the adjustment means includes a motor and reel arrangement. 43. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the adjustment means includes a plurality of motor and real arrangements for adjusting individual tensioning members attached to the plurality of nodes. 44. A heliostat as claimed in claim 42 or 43 wherein the adjustment means includes a controller for controlling the motor and reel arrangements such that the flexible member is displaced via the tensioning members into an orientation which ensures that sunlight is reflected to the target surface. 45. A heliostat as claimed in claim 44 wherein the controller is arranged in communication with a position sensing arrangement for sensing a position of each node of the flexible member relative a reference point. 46. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein a brake system is provided for applying a braking force on the motor and reel arrangement to stop reeling in or out of the tensioning members. 47. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein a stabilising means is provided for stabilising a reel of the motor and reel arrangement when torque is applied thereto by the motor. 48. A heliostat as claimed in claim 47 wherein the stabilising means is in the form of a rotating counterweight which is attached to the reel at an angle substantially perpendicular to the motor. 49. A heliostat as claimed in claim 47 wherein the stabilising means is in the form of cables which are attached to the reel at an angle perpendicular to the motor at one end and to the ground surface at an opposing end. 50. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein the target surface is in the form of a heat receiving collection device for a power generation system. 51. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein damping means is provided for damping and reducing resonance experienced by the reflective element, supporting structure or flexible member as a result of wind. 52. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein a windbreak system is provided for reducing the effects of wind on the flexible member, tensioning members or lifting means in use. 53. A heliostat as claimed in any one or more of the preceding claims wherein a retraction system is provided for retracting the flexible member into a retracted stowage condition when wind speeds exceed a predetermined maximum. 54. A heliostat as claimed in claim 53 wherein the retraction system includes a displacement arrangement for displacing the flexible member such that it folds and stacks on top of itself while being displaced into the retracted stowage condition. 55. A sunlight reflection system which includes a plurality of heliostats as hereinbefore described which are arranged to form corridors to direct wind between the heliostats. 56. A sunlight reflection system as claimed in claim 55 wherein a secondary heliostat is provided which is configured to receive and reflect sunlight from the plurality of heliostats on to the target surface. 57. A sunlight reflection system as claimed in claim 56 wherein the secondary heliostat is of a linear parabolic trough shape or a semi-circular parabolic trough shape.
